methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $149M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Valar Atomics is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised $149M to date. The nuclear energy sector comprises 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x multiplier to the $149M total raised proxy, yielding a $298M implied valuation. This method reflects typical capital step-ups from Series A to subsequent institutional funding rounds. Peer-median valuation could not be applied; while 8 same-sector private comparables exist, insufficient narrow-stage overlap prevented reliable triangulation—only 1 same-stage and 1 adjacent-stage peer met screening criteria, below the 3-datapoint threshold for robust median pricing.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $253M (low) to $343M (median), centered on $298M. This range reflects uncertainty inherent in a single-method framework. Key limitations include reliance on stage-progression mechanics rather than peer multiples, and the nascent state of nuclear venture comparables. Investors should note that nuclear sector valuations remain volatile and heavily dependent on regulatory, technical, and funding-environment shifts not captured in historical stage progression alone.
